steps.title: Identify the core contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts two time periods: “while the task was [blank] when few had been discovered, so many have since been discovered that the task has become nearly impossible.” The phrase “has become” signals a progression, and we need a word for the opposite of nearly impossible.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
“Feasible” means achievable or doable — the direct opposite of impossible.

When there were few planets, assigning unique names was feasible; as thousands more were discovered, the task became nearly impossible.

The other choices either address magnitude rather than achievability (substantial), contradict the progression by suggesting impossibility already existed (insurmountable), or concern clarity rather than difficulty (ambiguous).

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
